# Junyi Zhu

> Junyi Zhu is an AI researcher and Senior Applied Scientist at Microsoft, where he drives post-training for Microsoft 365 Copilot — post-training OpenAI models to synergize with Microsoft's tools, applications, and ecosystem, with a focus on enterprise and workplace scenarios. His research spans post-training of LLMs, generative models, federated learning, and privacy-preserving machine learning, with publications at NeurIPS, ICLR, CVPR, ICML, and EMNLP. He holds a PhD from KU Leuven (advised by Prof. Matthew Blaschko) and a Master's degree from the Karlsruhe Institute of Technology, and previously worked at Samsung Research.

Contact: via the email link on the homepage. Profiles: [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=3LeC4cMAAAAJ), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/junyi-zhu-ai/).

## Site structure

- [Home](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/): bio, news, selected publications
- [Publications](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/publications/): full publication list, generated from BibTeX
- Individual paper pages, linked from titles in the publications list (each has the abstract, a plain-language summary, key takeaways, BibTeX, and links to arXiv/code):
  - [Decoding Text Spans for Efficient and Accurate Named-Entity Recognition (ACL 2026)](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/publications/spandec/) — SpanDec is a span-based named-entity recognition framework built for industrial deployment, where latency and throughput matter as much as accuracy.
  - [Diffusion Alignment Beyond KL: Variance Minimisation as Effective Policy Optimiser (ICLR Workshop 2026)](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/publications/vmpo/) — VMPO reframes diffusion-model alignment through Sequential Monte Carlo: instead of optimizing a KL-based objective, it minimizes the variance of log importance weights along the denoising trajectory.
  - [Latent Zoning Network: A Unified Principle for Generative Modeling, Representation Learning, and Classification (NeurIPS 2025)](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/publications/latent-zoning-network/) — LZN asks whether one principle can unify generative modeling, representation learning, and classification: a shared Gaussian latent space in which each data type (images, text, labels) has an encoder mapping samples to disjoint latent zones and a decoder mapping back, so that tasks become compositions of encoders and decoders.
  - [Guided Model Merging for Hybrid Data Learning: Leveraging Centralized Data to Refine Decentralized Models (WACV 2025)](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/publications/guided-model-merging/) — Real-world data availability is often hybrid: abundant decentralized data on client devices plus a smaller curated centralized set at the server.
  - [Jigsaw-R1: A Study of Rule-based Visual Reinforcement Learning with Jigsaw Puzzles (TMLR 2025)](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/publications/jigsaw-r1/) — A DeepSeek-R1-style study of rule-based reinforcement learning for multimodal LLMs, using jigsaw puzzles as a fully label-free training signal: shuffling any image collection generates its own verifiable ground truth, with difficulty adjustable by grid size.
  - [Linear Combination of Saved Checkpoints Makes Consistency and Diffusion Models Better (ICLR 2025)](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/publications/lcsc/) — Training diffusion and consistency models discards a goldmine: the intermediate checkpoints.
  - [Efficient Expert Pruning for Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Language Models: Enhancing Performance and Reducing Inference Costs (arXiv 2024)](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/publications/efficient-expert-pruning/) — EEP compresses sparse Mixture-of-Experts LLMs with a two-phase, gradient-free evolutionary search: first prune to the best subset of experts, then "fine-tune" by merging the pruned experts' knowledge into the survivors — all with inference-only compute.
  - [FastMem: Fast Memorization of Prompt Improves Context Awareness of Large Language Models (EMNLP Findings 2024)](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/publications/fastmem/) — FastMem is test-time training for context faithfulness: before answering, the model briefly "memorizes" its prompt by minimizing next-token loss on it, updating only the last feed-forward module with a KL anchor to the original outputs.
  - [R-GAP: Recursive Gradient Attack on Privacy (ICLR 2021)](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/publications/r-gap/) — R-GAP established a third family of gradient privacy attacks: a closed-form, layer-by-layer recursion that reconstructs training data from gradients — the first such attack to work on both convolutional and fully connected networks, with or without bias terms.
  - [Confidence-aware Personalized Federated Learning via Variational Expectation Maximization (CVPR 2023)](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/publications/pfedvem/) — pFedVEM treats the shared model in personalized federated learning as a latent variable in a hierarchical Bayesian model, optimized with variational expectation maximization.
  - [Surrogate Model Extension (SME): A Fast and Accurate Weight Update Attack on Federated Learning (ICML 2023)](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/publications/sme/) — In real federated learning, clients share weight updates accumulated over many local steps — not per-step gradients — which was presumed to blunt gradient-inversion attacks.
  - [Rescaling Intermediate Features Makes Trained Consistency Models Perform Better (Tiny Paper@ICLR 2024)](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/publications/rescaling-consistency-models/) — A post-training tweak for consistency models: rescaling their intermediate features at inference time improves one-step sample quality with no detectable computational overhead, evidenced by a clear improvement in FID.
  - [Implicit Neural Representations for Robust Joint Sparse-View CT Reconstruction (TMLR 2024)](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/publications/inr-ct/) — CT machines routinely scan similar subjects — patients in a hospital, copies of an industrial part — yet implicit neural representations reconstruct each scan from scratch.
  - [Improving Differentially Private SGD via Randomly Sparsified Gradients (TMLR 2023)](https://junyizhu-ai.github.io/publications/dp-sgd-random-sparsification/) — Analyzing the convergence of differentially private SGD reveals a surprising property: randomly zeroing out gradient coordinates before clipping and noising can tighten the convergence bound when noise dominates.
